papers.title: HERMES++: Toward a Unified Driving World Model for 3D Scene Understanding and Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract HERMES++ combines 3D scene understanding and future geometry prediction through BEV representation, LLM-enhanced queries, temporal linking, and joint geometric optimization for autonomous driving applications.  AI-generated summary Driving world models serve as a pivotal technology for autonomous driving by simulating environmental dynamics. However, existing approaches predominantly focus on future scene generation, often overlooking comprehensive 3D scene understanding. Conversely, while Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate impressive reasoning capabilities, they lack the capacity to predict future geometric evolution, creating a significant disparity between semantic interpretation and physical simulation. To bridge this gap, we propose HERMES++, a unified driving world model that integrates 3D scene understanding and future geometry prediction within a single framework. Our approach addresses the distinct requirements of these tasks through synergistic designs. First, a BEV representation consolidates multi-view spatial information into a structure compatible with LLMs. Second, we introduce LLM-enhanced world queries to facilitate knowledge transfer from the understanding branch. Third, a Current-to-Future Link is designed to bridge the temporal gap, conditioning geometric evolution on semantic context. Finally, to enforce structural integrity, we employ a Joint Geometric Optimization strategy that integrates explicit geometric constraints with implicit latent regularization to align internal representations with geometry-aware priors. Extensive evaluations on multiple benchmarks validate the effectiveness of our method. HERMES++ achieves strong performance, outperforming specialist approaches in both future point cloud prediction and 3D scene understanding tasks. The model and code will be publicly released at https://github.com/H-EmbodVis/HERMESV2.

ru.title: Объединение разума и геометрии: мир автономного вождения через LLM и 3D
ru.desc: HERMES++ представляет собой унифицированную модель мира для автономного вождения, которая интегрирует понимание трёхмерной сцены и предсказание будущей геометрии в единой системе. Модель использует BEV-представление для консолидации информации с нескольких видов камер, совместимое с большими языковыми моделями, что позволяет передавать знания между задачами понимания и предсказания. Временной связи (Current-to-Future Link) обусловливает эволюцию геометрии семантическим контекстом, а совместная геометрическая оптимизация объединяет явные геометрические ограничения с неявной регуляризацией в скрытом пространстве. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют, что подход превосходит специализированные методы в задачах предсказания облаков точек и трёхмерного понимания сцены.

en.title: HERMES++: Bridging 3D Understanding and Future Predictions for Smarter Driving
en.desc: HERMES++ is a novel framework that enhances autonomous driving by integrating 3D scene understanding with future geometry prediction. It utilizes a Bird's Eye View (BEV) representation to merge spatial data, making it compatible with Large Language Models (LLMs) for improved reasoning. The model introduces LLM-enhanced queries to transfer knowledge effectively and employs a Current-to-Future Link to connect past and future geometric information. Additionally, a Joint Geometric Optimization strategy ensures that the model maintains structural integrity while aligning its predictions with real-world geometric constraints.

zh.title: HERMES++：自动驾驶的未来几何与三维理解的结合
zh.desc: HERMES++ 是一种结合了三维场景理解和未来几何预测的统一驾驶世界模型，专为自动驾驶应用设计。该模型通过鸟瞰视图（BEV）表示法整合多视角空间信息，并利用增强的大语言模型（LLM）查询来促进知识转移。HERMES++ 还设计了当前到未来的链接，以在语义上下文中调节几何演变，并通过联合几何优化策略确保结构完整性。经过多项基准测试的广泛评估，HERMES++ 在未来点云预测和三维场景理解任务中表现优异，超越了专业方法。

papers.title: MedSkillAudit: A Domain-Specific Audit Framework for Medical Research Agent Skills
papers.abstract: Abstract A domain-specific audit framework for medical research agent skills demonstrates reliable assessment consistency compared to expert review, supporting governance of specialized AI capabilities in healthcare applications.  AI-generated summary Background: Agent skills are increasingly deployed as modular, reusable capability units in AI agent systems. Medical research agent skills require safeguards beyond general-purpose evaluation, including scientific integrity, methodological validity, reproducibility, and boundary safety. This study developed and preliminarily evaluated a domain-specific audit framework for medical research agent skills, with a focus on reliability against expert review. Methods: We developed MedSkillAudit (skill-auditor@1.0), a layered framework assessing skill release readiness before deployment. We evaluated 75 skills across five medical research categories (15 per category). Two experts independently assigned a quality score (0-100), an ordinal release disposition (Production Ready / Limited Release / Beta Only / Reject), and a high-risk failure flag. System-expert agreement was quantified using ICC(2,1) and linearly weighted Cohen's kappa, benchmarked against the human inter-rater baseline. Results: The mean consensus quality score was 72.4 (SD = 13.0); 57.3% of skills fell below the Limited Release threshold. MedSkillAudit achieved ICC(2,1) = 0.449 (95% CI: 0.250-0.610), exceeding the human inter-rater ICC of 0.300. System-consensus score divergence (SD = 9.5) was smaller than inter-expert divergence (SD = 12.4), with no directional bias (Wilcoxon p = 0.613). Protocol Design showed the strongest category-level agreement (ICC = 0.551); Academic Writing showed a negative ICC (-0.567), reflecting a structural rubric-expert mismatch. Conclusions: Domain-specific pre-deployment audit may provide a practical foundation for governing medical research agent skills, complementing general-purpose quality checks with structured audit workflows tailored to scientific use cases.

ru.title: Автоматическая сертификация медицинских AI-навыков на уровне экспертов
ru.desc: В статье описывается MedSkillAudit — специализированная система оценки для проверки компонентов медицинских исследовательских агентов перед развертыванием. Система использует многоуровневый фреймворк для оценки научной целостности, методологической валидности и безопасности модульных навыков AI. Результаты показывают, что система достигает согласованности с экспертными оценками на уровне ICC(2,1) = 0,449, превышая межэкспертное согласие (ICC = 0,300), что демонстрирует надежность подхода. Исследование подтверждает, что предварительный предеплойментный аудит может служить практической основой для управления специализированными AI-возможностями в здравоохранении.

en.title: Ensuring Quality in AI: The MedSkillAudit Framework for Medical Research Skills
en.desc: This paper presents a specialized audit framework called MedSkillAudit for evaluating medical research agent skills in AI systems. The framework aims to ensure that these skills meet specific standards of scientific integrity and methodological validity before they are deployed. The study found that MedSkillAudit demonstrated a higher reliability in assessment compared to expert reviews, indicating its effectiveness in maintaining quality control. Overall, the framework provides a structured approach to govern AI capabilities in healthcare, enhancing the safety and reliability of medical research applications.

zh.title: 医疗研究代理技能的审计框架提升评估一致性
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种针对医疗研究代理技能的特定领域审计框架，旨在提高评估的一致性。该框架名为MedSkillAudit，能够在技能部署前评估其准备情况。研究表明，MedSkillAudit的评估结果与专家评审相比具有更高的可靠性，尤其在科学完整性和方法有效性方面。通过对75项技能的评估，发现57.3%的技能未达到有限发布的标准，显示出该框架在医疗AI应用中的重要性。
